title_en: The Doubt of the Mushrikeen: We Only Seek Intercession
title_id: Syubhat Kaum Musyrikin: Kami Hanya Mencari Syafaat
content_id:
Di antara keberatan-keberatan yang paling gigih yang diajukan oleh mereka yang menyeru kepada orang-orang suci, orang-orang saleh, dan mereka yang dimakamkan di kuil-kuil adalah klaim bahwa mereka tidak menyembah figur-figur ini — mereka hanya mencari syafaat mereka kepada Allah. Mereka berkata: kami tahu bahwa Allah adalah Pencipta dan otoritas tertinggi. Kami hanya ingin mendekatkan diri kepada-Nya melalui perantara-perantara yang dekat kepada-Nya. Apa yang salah dengan itu?

Keberatan ini bukanlah hal baru. Allah merekam argumen yang persis sama dari kaum musyrikin Arab kuno: 'Mereka yang mengambil pelindung-pelindung selain Dia berkata: Kami hanya menyembah mereka agar mereka mendekatkan kami kepada Allah' (Alquran 39:3). Dan dalam ayat yang sama pula, Allah membantah alasan ini secara tegas: 'Allah akan memutuskan di antara mereka tentang apa yang mereka perselisihkan. Sesungguhnya Allah tidak memberi petunjuk kepada pendusta yang kafir.'

Kaum musyrikin Arabia tidak meyakini berhala-berhala dan perantara-perantara mereka sebagai tuhan-tuhan dalam arti pencipta atau pemberi rezeki. Mereka tahu bahwa Allah menciptakan langit dan bumi dan bahwa hanya Dia yang mengendalikan hujan, kehidupan, dan kematian. Mereka berpaling kepada para perantara ini sebagai sarana untuk mendekati Allah — atau demikian klaim mereka. Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, diutus untuk mengoreksi pemahaman ini dan untuk memanggil mereka kembali kepada ibadah hanya kepada Allah.

Mengejutkan bagi mereka yang menyimpan keraguan ini, Alquran menjelaskan bahwa respons Nabi terhadap klaim ini bukan untuk menyetujuinya dengan koreksi kecil. Beliau menolaknya sepenuhnya sebagai syirik. Menyeru kepada siapa pun selain Allah — baik yang masih hidup maupun yang telah meninggal, manusia maupun malaikat — sebagai perantara dengan cara seseorang menyeru kepada Allah adalah tindakan ibadah yang hanya milik Allah. Masalahnya bukan konsep syafaat; syafaat ada dan merupakan rahmat nyata dari Allah. Masalahnya adalah bagaimana syafaat dicari.

Menurut Alquran dan Sunnah, syafaat hanya akan bermanfaat bagi mereka yang Allah izinkan untuk memberi syafaat dan hanya bagi mereka yang Dia ridhai. Tidak ada seorang pun yang memiliki kedudukan untuk memberi syafaat kepada Allah tanpa izin-Nya, dan tidak ada seseorang yang di dalam kubur yang memiliki kemampuan untuk merespons mereka yang memanggilnya. Allah berfirman: 'Dan siapakah yang lebih sesat daripada orang yang menyeru selain Allah — yang tidak akan meresponsnya hingga Hari Kiamat, dan yang tidak peduli dengan doa mereka?' (Alquran 46:5).

Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, membuat posisinya jelas tanpa keraguan selama hidupnya. Beliau memperingatkan putrinya sendiri Fatimah dan keluarganya sendiri bahwa garis keturunan dan hubungan dekat dengannya tidak akan bermanfaat bagi mereka jika mereka tidak memiliki amal untuk diri mereka sendiri. Beliau berkata: 'Jangan minta kepadaku; mintalah kepada Allah.' Beliau memberitahu para Sahabatnya untuk tidak menjadikan kuburannya sebagai tempat ibadah dan memperingatkan terhadap praktik-praktik yang mengarah kepada pengagungan kuburan.

Ada perbedaan krusial yang perlu dikenali: meminta orang yang masih hidup dan hadir serta mampu untuk mendoakanmu adalah diperbolehkan. Para Sahabat meminta Nabi untuk berdoa bagi mereka ketika beliau masih hidup. Tetapi menyeru kepada orang yang telah meninggal — mengarahkan doa kepada mereka, meminta mereka memenuhi kebutuhan-kebutuhan, mencari bantuan mereka dalam hal-hal yang hanya Allah yang dapat mengendalikannya — adalah hal yang sama sekali berbeda. Orang yang telah meninggal tidak dapat mendengar seruan-seruan minta tolong dalam arti yang diperlukan untuk ibadah, dan mengarahkan ibadah kepada mereka adalah definisi tepat dari syirik yang dilawan oleh para nabi.

Menghilangkan keraguan ini memerlukan kembali kepada makna fundamental tauhid al-uluhiyyah: semua ibadah milik Allah saja, dan syafaat dicari dari Allah, bukan dari para pemberi syafaat itu sendiri.
content_en:
Among the most persistent objections raised by those who call upon saints, righteous people, and those buried in shrines is the claim that they are not worshipping these figures — they are only seeking their intercession with Allah. They say: we know that Allah is the Creator and the ultimate authority. We simply want to draw near to Him through intermediaries who are close to Him. What could be wrong with that?

This objection is not new. Allah recorded the exact same argument from the Arab polytheists of old: "Those who take allies besides Him say: We only worship them so that they may bring us closer to Allah" (Quran 39:3). And in the very same verse, Allah refuted this reasoning decisively: "Allah will judge between them regarding that over which they differ. Indeed, Allah does not guide one who is a persistent liar and a confirmed disbeliever."

The polytheists of Arabia did not believe their idols and intercessors were gods in the sense of creators or providers. They knew that Allah created the heavens and the earth and that He alone controlled rain, life, and death. They turned to these intercessors as a means of approaching Allah — or so they claimed. The Prophet, peace be upon him, was sent to correct this understanding and to call them back to the worship of Allah alone.

Shocking as it may seem to those who hold this doubt, the Quran makes clear that the response of the Prophet to this claim was not to approve of it with a minor correction. He rejected it entirely as shirk. Calling upon anyone other than Allah — whether alive or dead, human or angelic — as an intercessor in the manner one calls upon Allah is an act of worship that belongs to Allah alone. The issue is not the concept of intercession; intercession exists and is a real mercy from Allah. The issue is how intercession is sought.

According to the Quran and the Sunnah, intercession will only benefit those whom Allah has permitted to intercede and only for those He approves of. No one has the standing to intercede with Allah without His permission, and no person in a grave has the ability to respond to those calling upon them. Allah says: "And who is more astray than one who calls upon other than Allah — one who will not respond to him until the Day of Resurrection, and who is heedless of their supplication?" (Quran 46:5).

The Prophet, peace be upon him, made his position unmistakably clear during his lifetime. He warned his own daughter Fatimah and his own family that lineage and close relationship with him would not benefit them if they did not have deeds for themselves. He said: "Do not ask me for things; ask Allah." He told his Companions not to make his grave a place of worship and warned against the practices that lead to shrine veneration.

There is a crucial distinction to recognize: asking a living person who is present and capable to pray for you is permissible. The Companions asked the Prophet to supplicate for them while he was alive. But calling upon the deceased — directing supplication to them, asking them to fulfill needs, seeking their help in matters only Allah controls — is a different matter entirely. The deceased cannot hear calls for help in the sense required for worship, and directing worship to them is the very definition of shirk against which the prophets were sent.

Removing this doubt requires returning to the fundamental meaning of tawhid al-uluhiyyah: all worship belongs to Allah alone, and intercession is sought from Allah, not from the intercessors themselves.
content_ar: Among the most persistent objections raised by those who call upon saints, الصالح people, and those buried in shrines is the claim that they are not worshipping these figures — they are only seeking their intercession with الله. They say: we know that الله is the Creator and the ultimate authority. We simply want to draw near to Him through intermediaries who are close to Him. What could be الخطأ with that? This objection is not new. الله recorded the exact same argument from the Arab polytheists of old: "Those who take allies بالإضافة إلى Him say: We only worship them لذا that they may bring us closer to الله" (القرآن الكريم 39:3). And in the very same الآية, الله refuted this reasoning decisively: "الله will judge between them فيما يخص that over which they differ. Indeed, الله does not guide one who is a persistent liar and a confirmed disbeliever." The polytheists of Arabia did not believe their idols and intercessors were gods in the sense of creators or providers. They knew that الله created the heavens and the earth and that He alone controlled rain, الحياة, and الموت. They turned to these intercessors as a means of approaching الله — or لذا they claimed. النبي ﷺ, ﷺ, was sent to correct this الفهم and to call them back to the worship of الله alone. Shocking as it may seem to those who hold this doubt, the القرآن الكريم makes clear that the response of النبي ﷺ to this claim was not to approve of it with a minor correction. He rejected it entirely as الشرك. Calling upon anyone other than الله — whether alive or dead, human or angelic — as an intercessor in the manner one calls upon الله is an act of worship that belongs to الله alone. The issue is not the concept of intercession; intercession exists and is a real الرحمة from الله. The issue is how intercession is sought. وفقاً لـ the القرآن الكريم and the السنة, intercession will only benefit those whom الله has permitted to intercede and only for those He approves of. No one has the standing to intercede with الله without His permission, and no person in a القبر has the ability to respond to those calling upon them. الله says: "And who is more astray than one who calls upon other than الله — one who will not respond to him حتى the Day of البعث, and who is heedless of their الدعاء?" (القرآن الكريم 46:5). النبي ﷺ, ﷺ, made his position unmistakably clear خلال his lifetime. He warned his own daughter Fatimah and his own الأسرة that lineage and close relationship with him would not benefit them إذا they did not have deeds for themselves. قال: "Do not ask me for things; ask الله." He told his الصحابة not to make his القبر a place of worship and warned against the practices that lead to shrine veneration. There is a البالغ الأهمية distinction to recognize: asking a living person who is present and capable to pray for you is permissible. The الصحابة asked النبي ﷺ to supplicate for them بينما he
